title: Executive Order 9379—Executive Order Establishing a Special Emergency Board To Report on the Adjustment of Wages for Employees of the Pacific Electric Railway Company of Los Angeles, California

flags.justification: A three-member board drawn from the established National Railway Labor panel, with its recommendations subject to review and potential override by the Economic Stabilization Director — a checked, layered process.
A specific, multi-recipient reporting requirement (the President, the Economic Stabilization Director, the National War Labor Board, and the Commissioner of Internal Revenue) with specific deadlines.
Single purpose despite a multi-step review process.
This uses already-established wage-stabilization criteria from the broader EO 9250 framework rather than creating new standards.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
A single-dispute, one-time board with a specific reporting deadline and implicit termination upon report submission.
This adds multiple layers of review (Board, then Economic Stabilization Director) rather than removing any.
Well-coordinated among the Board, the Economic Stabilization Director, and the National War Labor Board.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 0/10 = 0.0%. A well-designed, multi-layered wartime labor-dispute review mechanism.
